results.title: Case-hardening — Wikipedia
results.judge_reason: Source-domain encyclopedia entry on metallurgical case-hardening; describes only the metallurgical analog, contains no LLM/AI mapping or depth-graded alignment claim.

results.title: Carburizing — Wikipedia
results.judge_reason: Source-domain entry on interstitial carbon diffusion and case depth as f(T,time); purely metallurgical with no LLM application.

results.title: Carburization vs Case Hardening
results.judge_reason: Source-domain industrial comparison of carburization vs case-hardening process windows; no LLM connection.

results.title: Layer-Aware Representation Filtering (2025/7)
results.judge_reason: Identifies safety-sensitive layers and filters fine-tuning data, but does not impose a depth-gradient layer-weight schedule with a defined case-depth K over the last K layers.

results.title: SafeMERGE: Selective Layer-Wise Model Merging (2025/3)
results.judge_reason: Selectively merges LoRA layers based on cosine criterion at merge-time; not a training-time depth-graded loss-weight schedule and lacks the case-depth K analog.

results.title: DeepRefusal Beyond Surface Alignment (2025/9)
results.judge_reason: Probabilistically ablates the refusal direction across layers/token depths; the candidate explicitly distinguishes its depth-gradient schedule from this approach in novelty_claim.

results.title: Benign Fine-Tuning Breaks Safety in Audio LLMs (2026/4)
results.judge_reason: Descriptive empirical finding that late-layer LoRA updates suppress refusal in audio LLMs; not a prescriptive depth-graded safety fine-tuning schedule with case-depth K.

results.title: Layer-wise Alignment in VLMs (2024/11)
results.judge_reason: Analytical study of safety alignment across image-encoder layers in VLMs; does not propose a depth-graded loss-weight schedule for surface-only safety fine-tuning.

summary.fail_reason: 3 source-side keyword-overlap hits (Wikipedia Case-hardening, Wikipedia Carburizing, machinemfg comparison) trigger the pipeline rule (total_hits>=1). LLM-side results show no high-functional-equivalence prior art for the depth-graded layer-weight schedule with case-depth K, but the source-domain articles overlap on content_words by definition.
